title: The Freehold On The Plain
content:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  (Air: ן¿½The Little Old Log Cabin in the Lane.ן¿½)


ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Iן¿½m a broken-down old squatter, my cash it is all gone,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Of troubles and bad seasons I complain;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ My cattle are all mortgaged, of horses I have none,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And Iן¿½ve lost that little freehold on the plain.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  Chorus

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  The stockyardן¿½s broken down, and the woolshedן¿½s tumbling in;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  Iן¿½ve written to the mortgagees in vain;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  My wool it is all damaged and it is not worth a pin,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  And Iן¿½ve lost that little freehold on the plain.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ I commenced life as a squatter some twenty years ago,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ When fortune followed in my train;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ But I speculated heavy and Iן¿½d have you all to know
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ That Iן¿½ve lost that little freehold on the plain.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  Chorus: The stockyardן¿½s broken down, &c.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ I built myself a mansion, and chose myself a wife;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Of her I have no reason to complain;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For I thought I had sufficient to last me all my life,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ But Iן¿½ve lost that little freehold on the plain.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  Chorus: The stockyardן¿½s broken down, &c.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And now I am compelled to take a droverן¿½s life,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To drive cattle through the sunshine and the rain,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And to leave her behind me, my own dear loving wifeן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ We were happy on that freehold on the plain.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  Chorus: The stockyardן¿½s broken down, &c.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Banjo Paterson (Andrew Barton) represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Banjo Paterson (Andrew Barton)'s bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
